content:
✅ Approved the contribution.
Comment: Fun clash with an appropriate amount of difficulty :)

✅ Approved the contribution.
Comment: Fairly straight forward challenge, certainly something which can be achieved within the suggested "fastest" game mode. 

The difficulty, is easy.
There you go, I've made the changes for clarity.

For clarity, please standardise all instances of "water lilies" with "lily pads". You may also want to clarify that "the last lily pad" refers to the furthest lily pad from the frog's starting position. Thank you!

Binary output issue
* According to the contribution guidelines, binary outputs should be avoided for clashes. That's exactly the issue with your clash, as it allows only two possible answers (either "YES" or "NO"). Please modify the output requirement to allow for more than two possible answers.

Stub generator
* Please change Line 2 to use `loopline`, e.g. loopline N S:int.

Input
* Please use the variable name [[S]] (or another name used in the stub generator) to refer to each water lily position, and specify that [[S]] is an integer rather than a string.

Constraints
* Please add the lower bound for [[S]].

Cases
* Please renumber the cases so that the case numbers are in ascending order.

* Please remove all trailing newlines from the input.

Thanks.

Very neat concept; I like it! Here is some feedback:
1. Output: Fix the typo "Ligne 1" to "Line 1"
2. Statement: State that the frog's position starts at 0

That's all I have; some other reviewers might give you more feedback
Thank you for your feedback and the corrections I made to the statement.

(also to @j3r3m) I think an idea to avoid binary outputs is to print the 1-based index of the water lily that the frog stopped on. I also noticed how your test cases don't cover the maximum boundary of [[n]], which is 10, so try adding a test case that covers this maximum boundary. Also, I recommend maybe slightly increasing it as 10 "feels" a bit too small.
Missed your update @j3r3m. There is one more instance of "water lilies" in the last sentence of the problem description. Other than that, looks good to me :)

Thanks for your feedback. So, I've:

- Completed the problem statement to address the binary outputs.
- Modified Line 2 to make it a loop.
- Added the "nothing" output.
- Modified the constraints.
- Adapted the test cases accordingly.
- Reproduced the solution code.

Input
* Please change "The integers [[n]]" to "[[n]] space-separated integers".

Output / Cases / Solution
* Please change "invalid or inconsistent case" to "water lilies are not sorted in ascending order or there are duplicate positions".

* Consider changing "nothing" to "invalid" for clarity.

Constraints / Cases
* Test 2 and Validator 2 violate the third constraint. Please update the constraint and/or the cases.

Thanks.
There you go, sorry for the typos, I had platform errors on the previous submission (error #5xx) and I had to copy/paste my changes... Anyway, a bit of a mess!

No worries.

Output
* For clarity, please change "invalid or inconsistent case" to "water lilies are not sorted in ascending order or there are duplicate positions".

Cases
* Please renumber Test 7 and Validator 7 as Test 6 and Validator 6.

Thanks.

@VinceMC, what do you think of the new constraints/cases?

You really have a keen eye ! :)

Corrected !

Minor correction made.

Just a few more approvals.
(this is just for the notification, but I gave some further feedback through a reply to @5D1NL; check it for reference)
